Name: Agent workflow debugging
Description: Tracks agent tool calls and reasoning flows to troubleshoot multi-step decision paths in autonomous LLM systems.

Name: Agents
Description: Refers to autonomous assistants that orchestrate LLM calls, tool usage, and retrieval to complete complex workflows.

Name: Automation
Description: Focuses on automating routine work by combining LLMs with orchestration layers and infrastructure pipelines.

Name: Basic monitoring
Description: Covers fundamental health checks for latency, errors, and throughput before adding richer observability.

Name: Benchmarks
Description: Compares LLMs or workflows with consistent workloads to measure latency, accuracy, and safety.

Name: CI CD
Description: Applies continuous integration and deployment practices to LLM pipelines so models and prompts roll out safely.

Name: Content safety
Description: Covers alignment, filtering, and guardrails that keep generated content compliant with policy.

Name: Cost and performance
Description: Tracks inference costs and responsiveness to balance economic constraints with user experience.

Name: Data
Description: Refers to the structured and unstructured sources used to train, fine-tune, or ground LLM outputs.

Name: Data pipelines
Description: Describes ETL and preprocessing flows that feed cleaned, curated content into training, evaluation, or retrieval layers.

Name: Deployment
Description: Captures the steps for packaging, hosting, and routing inference workloads in production environments.

Name: Deployment and serving
Description: Combines deployment planning with API or UI endpoints that reliably serve LLM-generated responses.

Name: Distributed training
Description: Spans strategies that split model workloads across GPUs, nodes, or clouds to handle large LLMs.

Name: Ecosystem primitives
Description: Covers shared building blocks such as embeddings, evaluation suites, and agent patterns that power LLM applications.

Name: Evaluation and Testing
Description: Encompasses prompt evaluation, automated tests, and human review to verify model quality and safety.

Name: Experiment tracking
Description: Logs metadata, metrics, and artifacts from LLM experiments to compare runs and reproduce outcomes.

Name: Experiments
Description: Refers to structured trials that tweak prompts, data, or architectures to explore LLM behavior.

Name: Foundations
Description: Captures the core concepts of LLMOps such as data hygiene, prompt design, and deployment practices.

Name: Foundations and Lifecycle
Description: Describes the lifecycle of LLM projects from research through deployment and ongoing governance.

Name: Governance
Description: Includes policies, approvals, and audit trails that ensure responsible LLM usage.

Name: Governance and risk
Description: Combines compliance guardrails with risk modeling to anticipate harms and regulatory exposure.

Name: Lifecycle
Description: The end-to-end progression from idea through data, training, testing, deployment, and iteration.

Name: LLM Deployment
Description: Specifies strategies for packaging LLMs and their dependencies to run reliably in different environments.

Name: Model architecture
Description: Focuses on the neural structure, tokenization, and attention patterns that define an LLM.

Name: Model registry and versioning
Description: Tracks model artifacts, metadata, and version history so teams know which checkpoint is in use.

Name: Observability and tracing
Description: Uses telemetry, traces, and dashboards to explain every prompt, retrieval, and response in an LLM app.

Name: OpenAI-compatible endpoints
Description: Endpoints that mirror the OpenAI API surface so existing clients can target new hosts without code changes.

Name: Operations and maintenance
Description: Covers routine tasks like scaling, patching, and incident response for LLM services.

Name: Orchestration and workflows
Description: Defines pipelines that sequence retrieval, tool execution, and LLM calls to fulfill requests.

Name: PEFT
Description: Parameter-efficient fine tuning freezes most pretrained weights while adding a few trainable adapters for new tasks.

Name: Performance
Description: Measures throughput, latency, memory, and other signals to keep LLM services responsive under load.

Name: Post training
Description: Refers to steps like fine-tuning, calibration, or prompt tuning applied after base training completes.

Name: Post-training (QLoRA)
Description: Describes low-rank adapter fine tuning that updates only a small number of parameters for new tasks.

Name: Post-training (RLHF)
Description: Defines reinforcement learning from human feedback, which steers LLM outputs toward preferred behavior.

Name: Prompting
Description: Covers the design of instructions, examples, and context that guide LLM responses.

Name: RAG
Description: Retrieval-augmented generation interleaves document retrieval with LLM synthesis to ground answers in external sources.

Name: RAG workflow debugging
Description: Centers on inspecting retrieval hits, prompt composition, and agent steps when a RAG response fails.

Name: Regression testing
Description: Runs repeatable checks to ensure LLM behavior does not regress after updates or retraining.

Name: Reproducibility
Description: Ensures experiments can be rerun with the same data, code, and configuration to validate results.

Name: Retrieval augmented generation
Description: Adds authoritative knowledge context to prompts before synthesis so outputs reflect up-to-date facts.

Name: Safety
Description: Focuses on preventing hallucinations, bias, and unsafe outputs through filtering, red teams, and guardrails.

Name: Security configuration
Description: Configures authentication, encryption, and service permissions for safe LLM deployments.

Name: Serving integration
Description: Hooks LLM services into applications through orchestration of pre/post processing and APIs.

Name: Tracking
Description: Monitors metrics, budgets, and user feedback to steer ongoing improvements in the LLM lifecycle.

Name: Training
Description: Designs training runs, dataset curation, and optimizer schedules to improve model fidelity.
